zh.title: 雪崩币/美元技术分析：震荡偏弱整理格局
zh.content:
短期AVAX/USD整体处于震荡偏弱整理结构，价格在6.29附近徘徊，接近日内低点区域，多头动能有限，市场仍以区间运行为主。

MACD指标轻微金叉但动能柱较弱，短线多空力量接近平衡，RSI缺失导致超买超卖信号不完整，整体动量偏中性略显震荡。

下方关键支撑位于6.26附近SMA20均线区域，其次6.20形成更强防守；上方阻力集中在6.35至6.36前高区间，突破需放量确认。

操作上建议维持区间交易思路，在6.26-6.30低位分批布局多单，上破6.36可顺势追多，否则反弹至阻力区域考虑止盈。

en.title: Avalanche vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
AVAX/USD is currently in a tight consolidation phase around 6.29 after a modest 0.84% decline. Price action shows compression between 6.29 and 6.36, indicating reduced volatility and indecision. Despite intraday softness, the pair remains marginally above the 20-period SMA, suggesting that broader structure has not fully turned bearish. Market participants appear to be awaiting a directional catalyst. Volume contraction reinforces the sideways bias in the short term.

The MACD reading of 0.0102 indicates a faint bullish momentum, but the absence of a signal line reading limits confirmation strength. This suggests momentum is not strongly directional and may be stabilizing after recent pressure. Price trading just above the 20-period SMA at 6.265 reinforces a neutral-to-slightly bullish bias, though conviction remains limited without stronger trend expansion. This aligns with a broader consolidation rather than a trending environment.

Immediate support is located near the 20-period SMA at 6.265, followed by a more critical floor around 6.25. A breakdown below this zone could expose downside pressure toward 6.20. On the upside, resistance is defined by the recent high at 6.3582, with additional barriers near 6.40. A sustained break above resistance would signal renewed bullish continuation. This range remains technically compressed, favoring breakout setups.

Given the current structure, a range-trading approach is favored. Long positions may be considered near 6.25–6.27 with protective stops below 6.20 to manage downside risk. Profit targets can be set around 6.36 and 6.40 where resistance is evident. Alternatively, traders may wait for a confirmed breakout above 6.40 or breakdown below 6.25 before committing to directional positions. Patience is key in this low-volatility environment.
